text: I pick up on tension in a room before anyone says a word.
I can tell when someone is smiling politely but is actually upset.
I notice when two people in a group have unspoken friction between them.
I find myself reading between the lines in most conversations.
I often sense what someone really means even when their words say something different.
I detect shifts in a group's mood almost instantly.
I notice micro-expressions that flash across someone's face during conversation.
I can usually tell when someone is holding back what they actually want to say.
I intuitively understand the unspoken hierarchy in any social group I enter.
I feel like I have a sixth sense for when something is off in a social situation.
I notice when someone's body language contradicts their words.
I can read the atmosphere of a meeting within seconds of walking in.
I pick up on subtle vocal changes that signal someone is uncomfortable.
I often know what someone is about to say before they say it based on context cues.
I find that I naturally track the undercurrents of group dynamics.
I notice when someone is being left out of a conversation even when it is not obvious.
I sense when a compliment is actually passive-aggressive.
I pick up on the subtle power plays that happen in everyday interactions.
I can detect insincerity in someone's tone of voice even over the phone.
I notice when someone changes the subject to avoid an uncomfortable topic.

I sense when a conversation has an audience and the speaker is performing.

I pick up on which topics make certain people visibly tense.

I notice when people speak differently depending on who else is listening.

I find that I am constantly scanning faces for emotional information.
I notice the difference between someone who is quiet because they are content and someone who is quiet because they are upset.

I notice when someone positions a request as a suggestion to make it more palatable.

I sense the difference between a comfortable lull in conversation and an awkward one.

I am aware of the way seating arrangements reflect social dynamics.

I sense when someone is withholding information based on who else is present.

I detect the social undercurrents at a family gathering that newcomers would miss entirely.

I find that I read social situations the way a musician reads a score, perceiving multiple layers at once.
I believe that every interaction contains far more information than the words exchanged.
I pick up on the way people rearrange their expressions when they realize they are being observed.
I notice the subtle difference between someone who is listening and someone who is waiting to talk.
I am attuned to the way social norms shift from one context to another within the same group.
I prefer it when people say exactly what they mean.

I focus on the content of what someone says rather than how they say it.
